prompt: Design a distributed rate limiter capable of handling 100 million requests per minute across multiple data centers. Compare Token Bucket, Leaky Bucket, Sliding Window, and Fixed Window approaches. Recommend the best architecture, discuss CAP theorem implications, failure scenarios, consistency trade-offs, and provide high-level pseudocode.

You are given a legacy Python microservice with race conditions, deadlocks, memory leaks, and intermittent failures under high concurrency. Explain every issue you expect, propose a refactored architecture using async programming, provide corrected production-ready code, and explain why each change improves scalability.

Solve this logic puzzle: Five engineers (Alice, Bob, Carol, David, and Emma) each work on one unique project (AI, Cloud, Robotics, Security, and IoT), use one unique programming language (Python, Rust, Go, Java, and C++), and work in one unique city (Tokyo, London, Berlin, Toronto, and Bangalore). Given twelve constraints... [add 10–15 constraints]. Determine every engineer's project, language, and city, showing your reasoning step by step.

Write a production-ready Python implementation of an LRU Cache supporting O(1) get and put operations using a hash map and doubly linked list. Include unit tests, explain time complexity, space complexity, edge cases, and compare it with LFU Cache.

Compare PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, and DynamoDB for an event discovery platform expected to handle 500 million users worldwide. Discuss consistency models, indexing, replication, partitioning, query optimization, disaster recovery, costs, and provide a complete system architecture.

Explain how transformer attention works from first principles. Derive the scaled dot-product attention equation mathematically, explain why scaling is required, discuss multi-head attention, positional embeddings, and compare with recurrent neural networks.

Given a repository with 100,000 Python files, design an AI-powered static analysis engine capable of detecting security vulnerabilities, dead code, dependency cycles, and architectural violations. Describe the complete architecture, algorithms, data structures, indexing strategy, and complexity analysis.

Design a production-grade AI orchestration engine similar to OpenAI's internal routing system. It should dynamically select between local models, serverless models, and deterministic tools using confidence estimation, latency prediction, cost estimation, and historical routing statistics. Explain every subsystem in detail.
